title: calamagrostis varia subsp. corsica
intro: Calamagrostis varia subsp. corsica, also known as Corsican bent or Corsican fescue, is a sub-species of a perennial grass native to the island of Corsica. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to the limited scientific research available on its effectiveness.
